sections.paragraph:
ἀναντλέω (ἀντλέω ‘bail out bilge-water, draw water’) 1 aor. ἀνήντλησα; pf. ptc. ἀνηντληκώς lit. ‘draw up’ of water, drain out, empty fig., of dealing incessantly with toil or hardships bear patiently (Dionys. Hal. 8, 51 πόνους; Phalaris, Ep. 19 πόνους; Dio Chrys. 11 [12], 51; UPZ 60, 14 [168 b.c.] τοιούτους καιροὺς ἀνηντληκυῖα) ταῦτα πάντα 1 Cl 26:3 (Job 19:26). ἀνηντληκὼς κόπους Hs 5, 6, 2 (Bonner 58).—DELG s.v. ἄντλος.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

1 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Bonner Bonner = CB., s. H (=Hermas)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 69
